message:
fix(security): deny synchronous child_process exec by default

Disable synchronous subprocess execution (execSync, spawnSync,
execFileSync) by default to prevent extensions from bypassing capability
and risk mediation through direct synchronous process spawning. The new
allow_unsafe_sync_exec config field defaults to false; when denied, the
runtime returns a structured error response with code "denied" instead of
executing the command, and logs a warning event.

Also enriches the list_permissions() API to return full PersistedDecision
structs instead of bare booleans, preserving denial reasons, timestamps,
and other decision metadata for audit and UI display.

Changes:
- extensions_js.rs: add allow_unsafe_sync_exec gate to PiJsRuntimeConfig
  (default false), early-return denial response in __pi_exec_sync_native,
  add runtime_with_sync_exec_enabled() test helper, add denial-by-default
  test, update all existing sync exec tests to use enabled helper
- extensions.rs: list_permissions() now returns PersistedDecision map
  instead of HashMap<String, bool>
- tests/capability_prompt.rs: update list_permissions test expectations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
